titleStmt.title: 42. Memorandum From the President’s Special Assistant (Rostow) to Secretary of State ) to Secretary of State Rusk

div.head: 42. Memorandum From the President’s Special Assistant (Rostow) to Secretary of State Rusk Source: Johnson Library, National Security File, NSC History of the March 31st Speech, Vol. 7, Meeting Notes. Confidential.
div.p: Herewith a rough working outline of the headings for the Presidential speech about which we talked.

list.item: The Setting. A widespread, desperate and dangerous Communist effort along the whole front in Asia to divert us from Viet Nam, upset the progress made in Viet Nam, and discourage and split the American people.

Peace proposals.
—The San Antonio formula—as far as any American President can go: rock bottom.In a January 27 memorandum to the President, McPherson suggested eliminating the use of the term “San Antonio Formula.” He argued that “it might be hard for us if we had to accept a ‘Haiphong Formula’ or a ‘Vinh Formula.’” The President noted on the memorandum: “Harry, That’s fine. Bear that in mind in writing.” (Ibid., Office Files of Harry McPherson, Memoranda for the President (1968) [3 of 3])
—a serious intermediary has been trying to find out if the other side is prepared to accept it. I must conclude that they have not. Let us stop talking about a bombing cessation and keep clearly in our minds the bombings in Saigon, Danang, and all over South Viet Nam. When they are ready to talk about peace, they know where to get us. We shall be ready.

item.p: What are we seeking in Asia?A marginal note in an unknown hand referring to the next three paragraphs reads: “This is WWR not LBJ.”

list.item: We want an Asia in which both sides obey the law, both sides honor international agreements, and the nations in the area turn to their development in cooperation.
Call for unity and responsibility in the face of this Communist challenge despite an election year.
